title: Ket Maliss

{{Dialogue|Gyran|We are almost there.|Near-Human mercenary|Wake me when we have landed.|Maliss|<nowiki></nowiki>''[Snarls a similar warning.]''<nowiki></nowiki>|attr=Gyran, a [[Unidentified near-Human mercenary|mercenary]], and Ket Maliss as their team approaches the planet Sedri|src=<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />}}
'''Ket Maliss''' was a [[Dashade/Legends|Dashade]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] from the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] who was [[Suspended animation/Legends|cryogenically frozen]] for nearly 4,000 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] following the [[Cron Supernova/Legends|near-extinction]] of his [[Species/Legends|species]]. As a [[Shadow Killer]], Maliss was one of the most accomplished Dashade assassins; having originally worked as an enforcer for members of the [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] [[House Sizhran]], he was awoken several [[Time/Legends|decades]] before [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] to serve their descendant, [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]]. Under the Prince's employ, Maliss visited [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]] in [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine's]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] on at least two occasions. During the latter visit in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] curfew was imposed on the entire [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo system]]. A reluctant Maliss only left the establishment after [[Ackmena/Legends|Ackmena]], the [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]], prompted the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina's]] patrons to waltz out the door to her rendition of the [[Music/Legends|song]] "[[Goodnight, But Not Goodbye/Legends|Goodnight, But Not Goodbye]]."

By 3 ABY, Xizor had become the head of the criminal syndicate [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]], and that year saw Maliss wipe out the leadership of the [[Disac pirates]] on behalf of the Black Sun [[Vigo/Legends|Vigo]] [[Sprax]]. He later joined a team of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] assembled by Xizor to track down the mercenary [[Limna Yith]], who had stolen a [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] that contained a [[Barani List|list]] of every Black Sun [[operative]] in the [[Verde system/Legends|Verde system]]. The team pursued her to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sedri/Legends|Sedri]], where they learned that she was on her way to an [[Sedrian village|underwater village]]. Maliss and the others arrived first and [[Death/Legends|massacred]] the village's residents, but they were soon joined by Yith, held captive by a team of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] who were working in opposition to them. A [[Skirmish in the underwater caves|lightfight]] broke out that saw many of Maliss's allies killed, and the Dashade attempted to escape and fight another [[Standard day/Legends|day]].

==Biography==
===A deep sleep===
{{Quote|Just one more round, friend. Then homeward bound, friend. Don't forget me in your dreams.|Ackmena the bartender, singing to Ket Maliss and a group of other intoxicated aliens|<ref name="Holiday Special">''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]''</ref>}}
Ket Maliss was a [[Dashade/Legends|Dashade]]<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="blog WW" /> who lived during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]],<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" /> a massive conflict that pitted the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] against the [[Brotherhood of the Sith]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> The Dashade were renowned and feared throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as [[Death/Legends|deadly]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]],<ref name="AA">''[[Alien Anthology]]''</ref> and Maliss was one of the [[Species/Legends|species]]' [[Shadow Killer]]s,<ref name="Databank">{{DB|species|dashade|Dashade}}</ref> an honorary title given to the most accomplished Dashade assassins. The species' combat prowess saw them frequently hired by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], and cultures such as the [[Falleen/Legends|Falleen]] [[Species/Legends|species]];<ref name="AA" /> Maliss in particular worked as an enforcer for the Falleen [[House Sizhran]].<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />

[[File:Ket_maliss.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Ket Maliss sits in Chalmun's Cantina.]]
In the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3996 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> during the height of the war, the [[Cron Cluster/Legends|Cron Cluster]] of [[Star/Legends|stars]] went [[Cron Supernova/Legends|supernova]],<ref name="The Sith War 4">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 4]]</ref> and the nearby Dashade [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Urkupp]] was destroyed. The species was nearly [[Speciecide|annihilated]], but members of House Sizhran had Maliss and their thirty-seven other remaining Dashade enforcers [[Suspended animation/Legends|cryogenically frozen]]<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" /> so as to preserve their abilities for use by future generations of Falleen leaders.<ref name="AA" /> Roughly every [[Standard century/Legends|century]], a new enforcer was released and made to serve House Sizhran; Maliss was the thirty-eighth and final one, woken up several [[Time/Legends|decades]] before the year [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] to be a servant of the Falleen [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]],<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" /> a member of the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] criminal syndicate.<ref name="Jedi Twilight">''[[Coruscant Nights I: Jedi Twilight]]''</ref> Throughout those decades, Maliss served his master well<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" /> as the last Shadow Killer.<ref name="Databank" />

Late in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> Maliss was on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] to conduct lethal business in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]].<ref name="CCG">{{CCG|set=Premiere Limited|cardname=Ket Maliss}}</ref> While he was in the local [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]],<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> a skirmish at the bar occurred between the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and the outlaws [[Ponda Baba/Legends|Ponda Baba]] and [[Cornelius Evazan/Legends|Cornelius Evazan]].<ref name="A New Hope">{{Film|IV}}</ref> Maliss was again in the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] one evening<ref name="Holiday Special" /> midway through [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]<ref name="Leland Holiday">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=870#15778940|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2006-10-12|time=6:13 PM}}</ref> when an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (wallscreen)|officer]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] appeared on the establishment's [[wallscreen]] and announced a [[Tatoo system/Legends|system]]-wide curfew. Despite the urging of [[Ackmena/Legends|Ackmena]], the [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]], none of the cantina's patrons were eager to leave, and Ackmena reluctantly served a final round of [[Alcohol/Legends|drinks]] to everyone free of charge. She sang the [[Music/Legends|song]] "[[Goodnight, But Not Goodbye/Legends|Goodnight, But Not Goodbye]]" as she dispensed the drinks, and the serenade prompted Maliss and the other drunken denizens to waltz out the door. The evening's events were recorded and broadcast throughout the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] as a program called "[[Life on Tatooine]]," which was required viewing for all members of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]]. It was shown with the hope that viewers would be uplifted by a feeling of superiority over the cantina's lowly occupants.<ref name="Holiday Special" />

===On the hunt===
{{Quote|We're suiting up for wetwork!|Gyran, to Maliss and the rest of their team|<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />}}
Over the next few years, Maliss continued to work for Xizor, who by 3 ABY had become Black Sun's leader. In that year, Maliss was hired by the Black Sun [[Vigo/Legends|Vigo]] [[Sprax]] to deal with the [[Disac pirates]], who had long pillaged the [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] surrounding the [[Sisar Run/Legends|Sisar Run]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]]. When Sprax had attempted to subtly discourage them from continuing, the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] had responded by virtually shutting down the Sisar Run for three [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]], and Black Sun's profits in the region had subsequently suffered.<ref name="Sisar Run">''[[Secrets of the Sisar Run]]''</ref> Maliss killed [[Stano Hapin]], the group's leader,<ref name="GATORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> and a number of other high-ranking Disac pirates within a matter of [[Standard day/Legends|days]]. Sprax's [[Agent/Legends|agents]] were thus able to flush the leaderless group from the Sisar Run,<ref name="Sisar Run" /> but the Disac survivor [[Morturr Heth]] vowed revenge on both Maliss and Black Sun.<ref name="CSWE Heth">{{CSWECite|II|37|Heth, Morturr}}</ref> Sprax's personal [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]], [[Shotarr Kass]], also began to despise Maliss and view him as a threat.<ref name="Sisar Run" />

[[File:Gyranscrew.jpg|thumb|right|250px|ERYX-4, Gyran, and Maliss, while hunting for Limna Yith]]
Not long afterward, Maliss joined a team of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] and mercenaries gathered by Xizor to track down the [[Barani List]], which contained the names of every Black Sun [[operative]] in the [[Verde system/Legends|Verde system]]. A [[Datacard/Legends|datacard]] that housed the list had been stolen by the mercenary [[Limna Yith]], who then left a trail of destruction across the Sisar Run while fleeing from a team of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] hired by Vigo Sprax. When the Imperial [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Surlev]] followed her trail to the [[Abek's Station]] [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]] and [[Destruction of Abek's Station|destroyed]] the [[Space station/Legends|station]] after Yith was not turned over to him, Xizor assembled Maliss's team to work in opposition to Sprax's agents in order to speed up the chase and stave off any further Imperial interference in Black Sun affairs. Maliss boarded the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] [[Gyran]]'s ''[[Arc Razor]]'' and lazed listlessly around the passenger compartment with most of the team while Gyran and the [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] [[ERYX-4]] tracked Yith to the planet of [[Sedri/Legends|Sedri]].<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />

After picking an abandoned [[Imperial Aquatic Garrison|Imperial Garrison Base]] as Yith's most likely hideout, Gyran landed his [[Starship/Legends|ship]] outside of the base's [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] umbrella. The team then [[raft]]ed to their destination and scaled the walls of its [[Hangar/Legends|vehicle bay]]. When ERYX-4 detected a large heat signature, Maliss and the others followed it to an armory and discovered a large [[gweld]] creature, which they shot dead. The room also contained an empty lock box, which prompted concerns of a trap set by Yith; EYRX-4 hid a [[Listening device/Legends|listening device]] inside the box in case she returned. While they waited aboard the ''Arc Razor'', the device eventually picked up a conversation between Sprax's team of smugglers and a captive Yith who revealed that they were headed for a nearby [[Sedrian village]]. As the native [[Sedrian]]s were an aquatic species, the team donned [[wetsuit]]s and [[Breath mask/Legends|breath masks]] before pursuing. The team swam to the submerged village, massacred its residents and commenced searching for the datacard in the adjoining [[Water/Legends|underwater]] [[Cave/Legends|caves]], where they had to fend off [[Blood/Legends|blood]]-frenzied [[razort]]s.<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />

Yith and Sprax's team soon arrived in the caves with a group of Sedrian [[warrior monk]]s and were [[Skirmish in the underwater caves|attacked]] by four surviving villagers. Maliss and his team joined in the fray and lobbed [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] at both groups. Gyran and two others then provided covering fire while Maliss and ERYX-4 charged in to kill anyone who had been [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] by the explosions. They wanted Yith alive, but she proceeded to kill several members of Maliss's team with discarded Sedrian weaponry.<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />

{{RPG|"[[The Barani Conspiracy]]"}}
The surviving villagers were armed by Sprax's agents and joined in the [[lightfight]] on their side. Maliss eventually realized that the battle was hopeless, and he attempted to escape in order to fight another day.<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />
{{Endgame}}

After winning the battle, Sprax's agents continued their search. They soon recovered the datacard, which they were able to return to the Black Sun agent [[Kalend Thora]].<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Reckless bounty hunters have attacked a separatist village not too far from here.|The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]] [[Mors Odrion]], speaking of Ket Maliss's team on Sedri|<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />}}
[[File:Malissinwonderland.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Ket Maliss, Shadow Killer]]
Ket Maliss was a merciless warrior and a Dashade Shadow Killer<ref name="Databank" />—one of the most accomplished assassins among his species. During the Great Sith War, "Shadow Killer" was spoken only in whispers and gave those who heard it involuntary shivers;<ref name="AA" /> nearly 4,000 years later,<ref name="NEC" /> Prince Xizor still used it to denote Maliss.<ref name="CCG" /> One of Xizor's top operatives,<ref name="Sisar Run" /> Maliss served the Prince well for decades<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" /> and eliminated the leadership of the Disac pirates in a matter of days.<ref name="Sisar Run" /> While hunting for Limna Yith, he and his entire team massacred a Sedrian village that stood between them and their quarry.<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />

Maliss was the most feared member of the team assembled by Xizor to hunt for Yith. While the team was en route to Sedri, he warned Gyran with a snarl not to wake him frivolously; upon rising, he did not interact with his fellows and only stared forward with determination.<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" /> While drinking at Chalmun's Cantina, Maliss was one of many customers reluctant to leave in the wake of an Imperial curfew, but he joined in the line of patrons who danced out the door during Ackmena's song.<ref name="Holiday Special" /> Maliss had dark [[Color/Legends|green]] skin, red-orange [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and a [[Hair/Legends|hairless]] head.<ref name="The Last Page" /> As a Dashade, he could dissipate the heat emanated from his body and thus become difficult to detect with sensors.<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The Dashade started off as a nameless alien built for the cantina sequence in 'The Star Wars Holiday Special.' This fearsome creature is seen mingling in the famous Tatooine watering hole during a lively and seemingly impromptu musical number that clears out the establishment at closing time.|The [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]]|<ref name="Databank" />}}
[[File:Ketonset.jpg|thumb|right|174px|Ket Maliss on the set of ''The Star Wars Holiday Special'']]
Ket Maliss first appeared as a nameless alien drinking in the Mos Eisley Cantina in [[1978]]'s ''[[The Star Wars Holiday Special]]'',<ref name="Databank" /> in which he was played by an uncredited actor.<ref name="Fan site profile">{{WebCite|url=http://starwarsholidayspecial.com/characters/dashade.htm|text=Ket Maliss|work=[[Starwarsholidayspecial.com|The Star Wars Holiday Special]]|archivedate=20111014083341}}</ref> Although a number of the alien masks in the scene were re-used from [[1977]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|original ''Star Wars'' film]],<ref name="Roll call">{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/bts/article/f20030502/index.html|text=Cantina Roll-Call: Shedding Light on Some Alien Aliases|int=Cantina Roll-Call: Shedding Light on Some Alien Aliases}}</ref> Maliss's was built new for the ''Holiday Special''<ref name="Databank" /> under the supervision of makeup artist [[Rick Baker]]. The Cantina scene took twenty-four hours to shoot,<ref name="Starlog">[[Starlog (magazine)|''Starlog'']] Number 19, [[February]] [[1979]]: "[[Star Wars]]" Invades TV</ref> and some of the actors in alien suits passed out due to a lack of oxygen.<ref name="Comedy Hour 2">{{WebCite|url=http://www.vanityfair.com/magazine/2008/12/star_wars_special200812?currentPage=2|text=The Han Solo Comedy Hour! (page 2 of 3)|author=Frank DiGiacomo|date=2008-12-22|work=Vanity Fair|archivedate=20100829100918}}</ref> [[1979|One year later]], Maliss appeared in the background of a [[Star Friends|drunk driving public service announcement]] set in the Mos Eisley Cantina.<ref name="Don't drive drunk">''[[Star Friends]]''</ref>

Maliss was first identified by name in the [[1995]] ''[[Premiere Limited]]'' set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]]. The "Ket Maliss" card gave the character a brief backstory<ref name="CCG" /> and included a digitally manipulated photo that made it appear as if he were present in the Mos Eisley Cantina during the events of the original ''[[Star Wars]]'' film.<ref name="Databank" /> Maliss's backstory was significantly expanded in the [[1997]] sourcebook ''[[Secrets of the Sisar Run]]'', written by [[Craig Robert Carey]]; [[Shane Hensley]]; and [[Pablo Hidalgo]] for [[West End Games]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" /><ref name="Sisar Run" /> In one of the book's roleplaying scenarios, ''[[The Barani Conspiracy]]'', Maliss opposes the player characters in a lightfight that they must win, but the only clue given to his fate is that he attempts to escape if the battle seems hopeless.<ref name="The Barani Conspiracy" />
